records.ety: [OE. dignete, dignite, OF. dignet, dignit, F. dignit, fr. L. dignitas, from dignus worthy. See Dainty, Deign.]

defs: The state of being worthy or honorable; elevation of mind or character; true worth; excellence.

quotes: The dignity of this act was worth the audience of kings.

defs: Elevated rank; honorable station; high office, political or ecclesiastical; degree of excellence; preferment; exaltation.
quotes: And the king said, What honor and dignity hath been done to Mordecai for this?
Reuben, thou art my firstborn, . . . the excellency of dignity, and the excellency of power.

defs: Quality suited to inspire respect or reverence; loftiness and grace; impressiveness; stateliness; -- said of mien, manner, style, etc.
quotes: A letter written with singular energy and dignity of thought and language.

defs: One holding high rank; a dignitary.
quotes: These filthy dreamers . . . speak evil of dignities.
Sciences concluding from dignities, and principles known by themselves.
They did not stand upon their dignity, nor give their minds to being or to seeming as elegant and as fine as anybody else.
